name: Use Explanatory Variables
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 17: Smells and Heuristics (G19)
problem: Complex calculations or expressions are written as a single dense line, making it impossible to understand what intermediate values represent without mentally executing the arithmetic.
steps: Assign each meaningful intermediate value to a well-named local variable.
Read the variable names top-to-bottom as a plain-English summary of the algorithm.
Avoid combining assignment and usage in the same expression.
Ensure each explanatory variable has a name that makes a comment unnecessary.
bad_example:
// What does this compute?
return (order.getTotal() - (order.getTotal() * (customer.isPremium() ? 0.15 : 0.05))) * taxRate + shippingCost;
